title: Calonema reticulatum
taxonomy: Calonema reticulatum is classified as a species of orchid in the family Orchidaceae. It belongs to the genus Calonema, which is a small group of terrestrial orchids. The classification of Calonema reticulatum is further grouped under the subtribe Coelogyninae, which is a subcategory of the tribe Coelogyneae. Within the orchid family, Calonema reticulatum is most closely related to other terrestrial orchids native to tropical regions. The exact classification is as follows: Calonema reticulatum (Summerh.) Garay & G.A.Rinker.

intro: Calonema reticulatum, also known as Indian gentian or gentian of the Himalayas, is a species of flowering plant in the family Gentianaceae. It has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues, fever, and inflammation, due to its potential health benefits such as antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Calonema reticulatum include gentiopicrin, amarogentin, and loganin, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is commonly prepared as a decoction or a powder, and it is also used in the form of a tincture or an extract to treat various health conditions.
